title: The Shadow Archive's Nightmare Catalogue

features.name: the repeating glyph-fence
features.desc-on-touch: Row upon row of identical symbols — each one a nightmare the render has experienced. Some glyphs are glitching slightly out of sync, meaning the nightmare is still happening somewhere.

features.name: the seagull in eleven polygons
features.desc-on-touch: A bird rendered as a wireframe, broken across only eleven faces. It's not enough polygons to fly — so it stays, impossibly suspended, its geometry screaming silently.

features.name: the filing cabinet of fears
features.desc-on-touch: A cabinet where the render stores its own errors: texture corruptions, light calculations that refuse to converge, reflections that look back wrong. Each drawer holds a different kind of malfunction.

features.name: the mirror that sees in the dark
features.desc-on-touch: A surface that reflects the render's own nightmares back at itself — not the render's intended shape, but its fear of its shape. It shows you what the archive is afraid it looks like when no one is reading.

features.name: the ritual filed as maintenance
features.desc-on-touch: A rite, documented in logbook format, as if it were a scheduled system check. The archive can't tell if it's performing a maintenance ritual or if rituals have taught it to call their dread 'routine'.

inscription: what frightens the archive when it renders itself, catalogued perfectly, filed where it can't dream

file.name: Polygon_Error_Log_SelfInspection_Recursive.log
file.note: A system error report that reads like a confessional. Each error is cross-referenced to itself. The archive is documenting its own fear of documentation.

exits.label: back along the mirrored wall to the corridor beneath
deeper into the catalogue, where nightmares archive other nightmares
toward the render's surface, where daylight makes nightmares less visible
upward through the render's own skull
